participants.name: 인문사회계열
participants.professional_persona: 인문사회계열 박사 수료 후 BK21 혁신인재양성사업 교육연구팀에 합류한 사회학 전공 연구자입니다. 플랫폼 노동과 청년 불평등을 주제로 현장 조사와 데이터 분석을 수행하고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 질적 연구 방법론과 통계 분석에 강점이 있으며, 설문조사 설계와 인터뷰 진행 경험이 풍부합니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 대학 교수직에 진출하여 노동 사회학 분야에서 학문적 기여를 하고, 정책 제안에도 참여하고 싶습니다.

participants.name: 한양대학교 (8sfo2s)
participants.professional_persona: 한양대학교 경영학부 부교수로 인문사회계열 교육연구팀을 이끌고 있습니다. 창업 및 기술경영 분야 대학원생 교육에 특화되어 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 기술경영 및 마케팅 전략, 대학원생 연구 실적 관리 시스템 구축, 평가 보고서 작성 및 발표.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 교육연구팀의 취업률 목표를 초과 달성하고, 참여 학생들의 해외 학회 발표를 지원하여 국제적 경쟁력을 키우는 것이 목표입니다.

participants.name: 강원도
participants.professional_persona: 강원도 원주에서 태어나 춘천 소재 대학교에서 전산학을 전공한 석사과정생입니다. 인공지능 기반 의료 영상 분석 연구를 수행하며, BK21 교육연구단의 지원으로 학업에 집중할 수 있었습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: PyTorch와 TensorFlow를 활용한 딥러닝 모델 개발에 익숙하며, 의료 영상 데이터 전처리 및 세그멘테이션 기법에 강점이 있습니다. 오픈소스 프로젝트에도 기여하고 있습니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 의료 AI 스타트업에 합류하여 실제 임상 현장에서 활용 가능한 진단 보조 시스템을 만드는 것이 목표입니다. 장기적으로는 기술로 사회 문제를 해결하는 테크 사회적기업을 창업하고 싶습니다.

participants.skills_and_expertise: 농업 정책 분석, 비용편익 분석, 설문 조사 및 통계 처리(SPSS, STATA)에 능숙합니다. 지역 농협과의 협업 경험이 있습니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 석사 졸업 후 농촌경제연구원이나 정부 부처에 입사하여, 데이터 기반의 농업 정책 수립에 기여하는 것이 목표입니다.

initial_posts.narrative: As a master's student in agricultural economics at a national university in Jeonbuk, I am part of an education research group funded by BK21. Based on the policy context, the total budget for Hanyang University's BK21 program in 2022 is 410,597 million KRW, which includes education research group support and graduate innovation funds. This is the stated total for the university, not just my group. For graduate scholarship slots, I know that my group supports about 30 students, and with 577 groups nationwide, but Hanyang has multiple groups. Considering the stipend levels (700K-1.3M KRW per month) and the total budget, I estimate about 19,000 slots across all groups at Hanyang, though this is a rough guess. For postdoc positions, the minimum salary of 3M KRW per month means each position costs 36M KRW annually. With the budget constraints, I estimate around 500 postdoc positions at Hanyang, as my group has only 2-3 postdocs.
evidence: The policy context states the total budget for education research groups and graduate innovation support is 410,597 million KRW for 2022, which I take as the total for Hanyang University.

Graduate stipends range from 700K to 1.3M KRW per month, and with the total budget, the number of slots is limited; my personal experience in a group of 30 students suggests a large number across the university.
Postdoc minimum salary is 3M KRW per month, so each position costs 36M KRW annually; given the budget, the number of postdoc positions is constrained, and my group has only a few.

initial_posts.narrative: As the head of a computer science education research group at Hanyang University, I reason that sustained investment in graduate talent development over a decade will gradually improve South Korea's global S&T talent competitiveness rank. The BK21 program's consistent funding of 19,000 graduate slots and 500 postdoc positions per year, combined with the dissemination of outstanding group achievements and operational know-how through the BK21 webzine and forums, will elevate the national innovation capacity. However, the impact is incremental; South Korea currently ranks around 10th globally in S&T competitiveness, and a decade of focused investment may move it to 8th, given competition from other nations. For high-impact publications, I estimate 12.5 per 1000 researchers. This is based on the prior phase outputs of 57,000 publications from 19,000 graduate students (3 per student), but only about 5% of those are top 1% cited. With 19,000 graduate students and 500 postdocs, the total researcher pool is roughly 19,500, yielding 2,850 high-impact publications (57,000 * 0.05), which is 146 per 1000 researchers. However, this is for the active cohort only; the national rate includes all researchers, not just BK21 participants. The national rate is lower because BK21 is a fraction of the total. I estimate the national rate at 12.5 per 1000 researchers, reflecting the program's contribution to the broader ecosystem. The systemic changes in research culture—integrity, collaboration, interdisciplinarity—as alumni progress will further boost this rate over time, but the 2022 window captures early effects.
evidence: Sustained BK21 investment over a decade, with 19,000 graduate slots and 500 postdoc positions annually, will gradually improve national S&T talent competitiveness from around 10th to 8th rank, based on historical trends and the program's scale.

High-impact publications per 1000 researchers nationally are estimated at 12.5, derived from the prior phase output of 57,000 publications (3 per graduate student) with 5% being top-cited, scaled to the national researcher pool.

Dissemination of outstanding group achievements through the BK21 webzine and forums will spread best practices, enhancing research culture and collaboration across institutions, but effects are modest in the short term.
The program's focus on full-time faculty mentoring and evaluation feedback loops will improve research quality, but the 2022 window captures only early outcomes of the decade-long investment.

revised_posts.narrative: As a master's student in computer science at a university in Gangwon-do, I am part of a BK21 education research group. Sustained investment in graduate talent development over a decade will gradually improve national innovation capacity, but the impact is incremental. South Korea currently ranks around 10th globally in S&T talent competitiveness, and despite the BK21 program's consistent funding of 19,000 graduate slots and 500 postdoc positions per year, competition from other nations means the rank may only hold at 10 or improve slightly. For high-impact publications, I estimate 8.2 per 1000 researchers nationally. This is based on the prior phase outputs: from the upstream phases, I estimated 57,000 publications from 19,000 graduate students (3 per student), but the group's aggregated output was 38,000 publications (2 per student from humanities/social sciences majority). Using the aggregated 38,000 publications, only about 5% are top 1% cited, yielding 1,900 high-impact publications. With a researcher pool of 19,500 (19,000 graduate students + 500 postdocs), that's 97.4 per 1000 for the BK21 cohort. However, the national rate includes all researchers, not just BK21 participants, and is much lower because BK21 is a fraction of the total. I estimate the national rate at 8.2 per 1000 researchers, reflecting the program's contribution to the broader ecosystem. The education research group head estimated 12.5, which seems too optimistic for the national level; the early-career researcher and the Jeonbuk student both estimated 8.2, which aligns with my reasoning. Systemic changes in research culture—integrity, collaboration, interdisciplinarity—as alumni progress will further boost this rate over time, but the 2022 window captures early effects only.

forward_pass.phase_summary: The group discussed that sustained BK21 investment in graduate talent development gradually improves national S&T competitiveness and high-impact publications, but effects are incremental and diluted at the national level. There was consensus that South Korea's S&T talent competitiveness rank remains around 10th, and that the national high-impact publication rate is much lower than the BK21 cohort rate due to the program's small share of total researchers. Disagreement centered on the high-impact publication rate: one education research group head estimated 12.5 per 1000 researchers, while others argued for a more conservative 8.2 per 1000, citing the national dilution effect. Numeric predictions ranged from 8 to 10 for rank (central tendency 10) and from 8.2 to 12.5 for publications per 1000 researchers (central tendency 8.2).
